description:
An aerodynamicist is building a VSPAero drag database for a variable-sweep interceptor concept. The provided baseline model is in the takeoff configuration (unswept) at /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/fx_unswept.vsp3. Before supersonic wave drag analysis can begin, the model must be accurately updated to the swept-back dash configuration.

Read the specification document at /home/ga/Desktop/swing_wing_spec.txt. It contains the target leading-edge sweep angle. However, because OpenVSP's 'Span' parameter defines the projected Y-axis span, simply changing the sweep angle will artificially stretch the physical length of the wing panel.

You must calculate the new projected span required to maintain the exact same physical leading-edge panel length as the baseline 20-degree swept wing. Then, open the model in OpenVSP, update the Wing component's Leading Edge Sweep and Total Span to your calculated values, and save the modified model as /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/fx_swept.vsp3.
